title: Artemis II launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Artemis II was a crewed flyby of the Moon.

title: Apollo 11 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 11 was the American spaceflight that first landed humans on the Moon, and the fifth crewed mission of NASA's Apollo program.

title: Apollo 13 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 13 was the seventh crewed mission in the Apollo space program and would have been the third Moon landing.

title: Artemis III launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Artemis III is planned to be the second crewed mission of the NASA-led Artemis lunar exploration program.

title: Apollo 17 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 17 was the eleventh and final crewed mission of NASA's Apollo program, the sixth and most recent time humans have set foot on the Moon.

title: Artemis I launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Artemis I, formerly Exploration Mission-1 (EM-1), was an uncrewed Moon-orbiting mission that launched on November 16, 2022.

title: Apollo 8 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 8 was the first crewed spacecraft to leave Earth's gravitational sphere of influence, and the first human spaceflight to reach the Moon.

title: Chandrayaan-3 launches
subtitle: Mission by Indian Space Research Organisation
fact: Chandrayaan-3 is the third mission in the Chandrayaan programme, a series of lunar-exploration missions developed by ISRO.

title: Apollo 10 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 10 was the fourth human spaceflight in the United States' Apollo program and the second to orbit the Moon.

title: Apollo 9 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 9 was the third human spaceflight in NASA's Apollo program, which successfully tested systems and procedures critical to landing on the Moon.

title: Apollo 12 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 12 was the sixth crewed flight in the United States Apollo program and the second to land on the Moon.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches
subtitle: Space mission
fact: Shenzhou 23 is an ongoing Chinese spaceflight to transport three taikonauts on a Shenzhou spacecraft to and from the Tiangong space station.

title: Cassini−Huygens launches
subtitle: Space mission
fact: Cassini–Huygens, commonly called Cassini, was a space-research mission by NASA, the European Space Agency (ESA), and the Italian Space Agency (ASI) to send a space probe to study the planet Saturn and its system, including its rings and natural satellites.

title: Apollo 15 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 15 was the ninth crewed mission in the Apollo program and the fourth Moon landing.

title: Soyuz 11 launches
subtitle: Mission by Soviet space program
fact: Soyuz 11 was the only crewed mission to board the world's first space station, Salyut 1.

title: Apollo 14 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 14 was the eighth crewed mission in the United States Apollo program, the third to land on the Moon, and the first to land in the lunar highlands.

title: Gemini 8 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Gemini 8 was the sixth crewed spaceflight in NASA's Gemini program.

title: Apollo 16 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 16 was the tenth crewed mission in the United States Apollo space program, administered by NASA, and the fifth and penultimate to land on the Moon.

title: Vostok 1 launches
subtitle: Mission by Soviet space program
fact: Vostok 1 was the first spaceflight of the Vostok programme and the first human orbital spaceflight in history.

title: Apollo 7 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Apollo 7 was the first crewed flight in NASA's Apollo program, and saw the resumption of human spaceflight by the agency after the fire that had killed the three Apollo 1 astronauts during a launch rehearsal test on January 27, 1967.

title: Double Asteroid Redirection Test launches
subtitle: Mission by The Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory LLC
fact: The Double Asteroid Redirection Test (DART) was a NASA space mission to test a method of planetary defense against near-Earth objects (NEOs).

subtitle: Mission by Soviet space program
fact: Soyuz 1 was a crewed spaceflight of the Soviet space program.

title: Mercury-Atlas 6 launches

fact: Mercury-Atlas 6 (MA-6) was the first crewed American orbital spaceflight, which took place on February 20, 1962.

subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-51-L was the disastrous 25th mission of NASA's Space Shuttle program and the final flight of Space Shuttle Challenger.

fact: BepiColombo is a joint mission of the European Space Agency (ESA) and the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) to the planet Mercury.

fact: OSIRIS-REx was a NASA asteroid-study and sample-return mission that visited and collected samples from 101955 Bennu, a carbonaceous near-Earth asteroid.

fact: STS-1 was the first orbital spaceflight of NASA's Space Shuttle program.

subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Mercury-Redstone 3, or Freedom 7, was the first United States human spaceflight, on May 5, 1961, piloted by astronaut Alan Shepard.

title: Gemini 2 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Gemini 2 was the second spaceflight of the American human spaceflight program Project Gemini, and was launched and recovered on January 19, 1965.

title: Mars Cube One launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Mars Cube One was a Mars flyby mission launched on 5 May 2018 alongside NASA's InSight Mars lander.

title: Saturn-Apollo 5 launches
subtitle: Space mission
fact: Saturn-Apollo 5 (SA-5) was the first launch of the Block II Saturn I rocket and was part of the Apollo program.

title: Axiom Mission 3 launches
subtitle: Mission by SpaceX
fact: Axiom Mission 3 was a private spaceflight to the International Space Station.

title: STS-63 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-63 was the second mission of the U.S.-Russian Shuttle–Mir program and the 20th flight of Space Shuttle Discovery.

title: STS-35 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-35 was the tenth flight of Space Shuttle Columbia, in the 38th shuttle mission.

title: Blue Origin NS-16 launches
subtitle: Mission by Blue Origin
fact: Blue Origin NS-16 was a sub-orbital spaceflight mission operated by Blue Origin which flew on 20 July 2021.

title: STS-29 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-29 was the 28th NASA Space Shuttle mission, during which Space Shuttle Discovery inserted a Tracking and Data Relay Satellite (TDRS) into Earth orbit.

title: Soyuz TM-13 launches
subtitle: Mission by Soviet space program
fact: Soyuz TM-13 was the 13th expedition to the Mir space station.

title: STS-60 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-60 was the first mission of the U.S.

title: STS-34 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-34 was a NASA Space Shuttle mission using Atlantis.

title: STS-99 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-99 was a Space Shuttle mission using Endeavour, that launched on 11 February 2000 from Kennedy Space Center, Florida.

title: STS-37 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-37, the thirty-ninth NASA Space Shuttle mission and the eighth flight of the Space Shuttle Atlantis, was a six-day mission with the primary objective of launching the Compton Gamma Ray Observatory (CGRO), the second of the Great Observatories program which included the visible-spectrum Hubble Space Telescope (HST), the Chandra X-ray Observatory (CXO) and the infrared Spitzer Space Telescope.

title: STS-61-B launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-61-B was the 23rd NASA Space Shuttle mission, and its second using Space Shuttle Atlantis.

title: Starship Flight Test 2 launches
subtitle: Mission by SpaceX
fact: Starship flight test 2 was the second flight test of the SpaceX Starship launch vehicle.

title: STS-45 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-45 was a 1992 NASA Space Shuttle mission using the Space Shuttle Atlantis.

title: STS-67 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-67 was a human spaceflight mission using Space Shuttle Endeavour that launched from Kennedy Space Center, Florida on March 2, 1995.

title: STS-51 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-51 was a NASA Space Shuttle Discovery mission that launched the Advanced Communications Technology Satellite (ACTS) in September 1993.

title: STS-74 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-74 was the fourth mission of the US/Russian Shuttle–Mir program, and the second docking of the Space Shuttle with Mir.

title: STS-92 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-92 was a Space Shuttle mission to the International Space Station (ISS) flown by Space Shuttle Discovery.

title: STS-33 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-33 was a NASA Space Shuttle mission and the 9th flight of Discovery, during which Space Shuttle Discovery deployed a payload for the United States Department of Defense (DoD).

title: STS-42 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-42 was a NASA Space Shuttle Discovery mission with the Spacelab module.

title: STS-80 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-80 was a Space Shuttle mission flown by Space Shuttle Columbia.

title: Axiom Mission 2 launches
subtitle: Mission by SpaceX
fact: Axiom Mission 2 was a private crewed spaceflight operated by Axiom Space.

title: STS-39 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-39 was the twelfth mission of the NASA Space Shuttle Discovery, and the 40th orbital shuttle mission overall.

title: Big Joe 1 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: Big Joe 1 (Atlas-10D) launched an uncrewed boilerplate Mercury capsule from Cape Canaveral, Florida on 9 September 1959.

title: STS-36 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-36 was a NASA Space Shuttle mission, during which Space Shuttle Atlantis carried a classified payload for the U.S.

title: Soyuz TMA-20 launches
subtitle: Mission by Roscosmos State Corporation
fact: Soyuz TMA-20 was a human spaceflight to the International Space Station (ISS) and was part of the Soyuz programme.

title: STS-89 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-89 was a Space Shuttle mission to the Mir space station flown by Space Shuttle Endeavour, and launched from Kennedy Space Center, Florida on 22 January 1998.

title: STS-41 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-41 was the 36th Space Shuttle mission and the eleventh mission of the Space Shuttle Discovery.

title: Soyuz MS-12 launches
subtitle: Mission by Roscosmos State Corporation
fact: Soyuz MS-12 was a Soyuz spaceflight that launched on 14 March 2019, carrying three members of the Expedition 59 crew to the International Space Station.

title: STS-91 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-91 was the 24th flight of Discovery, and the final Space Shuttle mission to the Mir space station.

title: STS-30 launches
subtitle: Mission by National Aeronautics and Space Administration
fact: STS-30 was the 29th NASA Space Shuttle mission and the fourth mission for Space Shuttle Atlantis.

title: Soyuz 3 launches
subtitle: Mission by Soviet space program
fact: Soyuz 3 was a spaceflight mission launched by the Soviet Union on 26 October 1968.

title: Soyuz TM-21 launches
subtitle: Mission by Roscosmos State Corporation
fact: Soyuz TM-21 was a crewed Soyuz spaceflight to Mir.

title: Vostok 5 launches
subtitle: Mission by Soviet space program
fact: Vostok 5 was a joint mission of the Soviet space program together with Vostok 6; as with the previous pair of Vostok 3 and Vostok 4 the two Vostok spacecraft came close to one another in orbit and established a radio link.
